title: Revision
prose: The last fortnight of term turns the whole castle strange: the library fuller than you've ever seen it, older students muttering mnemonics under their breath in corridors, a general low hum of anxiety that even the portraits seem to have picked up on.

Your own exams are hardly the N.E.W.T.s everyone keeps darkly referencing, but they're real enough — written papers for most subjects, and a practical for Charms and Transfiguration where Flitwick and McGonagall watch, clipboard in hand, while you try to remember which wand movement goes with which spell under the worst possible pressure.
By the time the last exam ends, the collective sound the year group makes in the entrance hall is somewhere between a cheer and a whimper.

choices.label: You revised properly, and it showed.
You mostly winged it and hoped for the best.
